title: Cryptantha aspera
intro: Cryptantha aspera, also known as rough cryptantha, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cryptantha aspera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Cryptantha aspera is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to treat conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Cryptantha aspera is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a member of the genus Cryptantha, which comprises over 200 species of plants. The genus Cryptantha is part of the tribe Cynoglosseae, and the family Boraginaceae is a group of flowering plants that includes over 2,000 species. The classification of Cryptantha aspera places it within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
